title: The Motel's First Night, Never-Ending

features.name: the-first-room
features.desc-on-touch: the motel room where night always arrives at the same moment, the sheets crisp with an eternity of first-time newness

features.name: the-neon-reflection
features.desc-on-touch: the motel sign's glow pools on the asphalt; it never dims, waiting for guests who only ever arrive once

features.name: the-stack-of-nights
features.desc-on-touch: each night stacked atop the last, identical, unseparable; the archive counting something it cannot divide

features.name: the-clock-that-stopped-arriving
features.desc-on-touch: the bedside hour, frozen between dusk and the full dark; time bent into a permanent moment of approach

features.name: the-ledger-entry
features.desc-on-touch: the desk register; one name written in an infinite loop, each copy aged to perfection simultaneously

inscription: the night that came to stay, because it had nowhere else to arrive

file.name: FirstNight_AudioLog_Compressed.mp3
file.note: the motel's opening night: television hum, ice machine, traffic on the highway that never changes; STATUS: germinating — grows older without time passing

reverie.title: REVERIE — the desk where guests never arrived
narr: you are both at the front desk, the ledger open.
she traces the first entry with one finger; the ink
has aged forty years in a single night. the neon sign
outside bleeds through the window onto the page.
her hand moves to the next line, identical, and the
next. she says: the motel is still checking them in.

exits.label: back up through the floors, through years that all feel like the first
along the corridor to the other rooms, each one the same room at the same moment
down into the basement where the motel's boiler counts infinite seconds
